title: DSDS Theme Definition
description: A theme provides alternative token values for a named context such as a color mode, density setting, or brand variant. Themes reference existing tokens and supply override values without redefining the token's identity, docs, or API mappings. This keeps the token catalog as the single source of truth for what a token is. Themes describe how token values change across contexts. Express internal artifact relationships through the unified `links` array rather than a dedicated guideline type.

tokenOverride.description: An alternative value for a single token within a theme. Maps a token name to its theme-specific value.

token.description: The name of the token being overridden. MUST match the `name` of a token defined elsewhere in the system. Token names are unconstrained by a pattern to support DTCG and design tool naming conventions (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary').

value.description: The override value for this token within the theme. Follows the same structure as token.value — a displayable representation containing a resolved value, alias reference, and/or DTCG file pointer.

theme.description: Docs for a theme — a named set of token value overrides that adapt the design system to a specific context. Use themes for color modes (light, dark, high-contrast), density settings (compact, comfortable), brand variants, or product-specific customizations. A theme does not redefine tokens; it references existing tokens and provides alternative values. The token catalog remains the authoritative source for token identity, type, docs, and API mappings.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a theme.

displayName.description: Human-readable name for the theme (e.g., 'Dark Mode', 'High Contrast', 'Compact Density').

aliases.description: Alternative names for this artifact across teams, tools, or legacy systems. Useful for search, migration mapping, and cross-referencing.

summary.description: A one-line, plain-text summary for compact display contexts such as list views, tooltips, search results, and cards. MUST NOT contain markup.

thumbnail.description: A visual for compact display contexts such as list views, tooltips, search results, and cards.

description.description: A description of the theme — what context it adapts the system to, when to apply it, and how it differs from default token values. CommonMark supported.

preview.description: A visual or interactive preview — a hero image, video walkthrough, code snippet, or link to a live demo. Accepts any presentation type from the presentation schema.

since.description: The design system version in which this theme was introduced.

tags.description: Freeform keywords for categorization and search (e.g., 'dark-mode', 'accessibility', 'brand').

category.description: The category of adaptation this theme represents. Common values: 'color-mode' (light/dark/high-contrast), 'density' (compact/comfortable/spacious), 'brand' (sub-brand or partner theming), 'product' (product-specific overrides). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case.

overrides.description: Token value overrides that define this theme. Each entry maps a token name to its theme-specific value. Only list tokens whose values differ from the default — unlisted tokens retain their default values.

guidelines.description: All structured docs for this theme as an ordered array of typed guideline objects. Themes accept general guideline types only (best-practices, use-cases, accessibility, examples). Each guideline has a `type` property that determines its shape. Ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display.

links.description: External resource links, related artifact references, and internal relationships. Each link can point to an external URL, an internal DSDS artifact by name, or both.
